name: Newport Indoor Golf
about: Newport Indoor Golf is a premier indoor golfing facility located in Portsmouth, Rhode Island. The venue features a 6,000 square foot space equipped with 8 Trackman simulator bays, one private fitting bay, and two private group bays. Guests can access over 250 world-renowned golf courses and utilize a dual-function driving range, regardless of weather conditions. The facility offers a comprehensive suite of services including one-on-one lessons with PGA professionals, expert club fittings using Trackman technology, a pro shop with top-tier brands, and certified club repair services. Newport Indoor Golf is an all-season destination for golfers of all skill levels to practice, play, and improve their game in a climate-controlled environment.
why_golfers_like_it: State-of-the-art Trackman technology for precise swing analysis
Access to over 250+ world-renowned golf courses
Comprehensive services including lessons, fittings, and repairs
Private bays available for group outings and focused training

food_and_drink.notes: No food service mentioned on website
No bar or alcohol service mentioned; BYOB not specified

simulator_hardware.notes: 8 bays equipped with Trackman technology, one private fitting bay, and two private group bays

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: You are welcome to bring your own clubs. The facility also has a pro shop where you can rent or purchase clubs from top brands like Titleist, Ping, Taylormade, Callaway, and more.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not specify a policy on outside food and drink. No on-site bar or kitchen service is mentioned, so it's advisable to contact the venue directly for clarification.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: You can book up to 3 hours online for a bay reservation. For extended time or to play a full 18 holes, it is best to contact the front desk directly.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: There is no specific information on child-friendly features or junior programs. While families are mentioned as a target audience, the focus appears to be on adult golfers and serious practice.
